metadata.name: veilpay
metadata.description: VeilPay MPC | confidential payments with Arcium

instructions.name: debit
docs: Queue a confidential debit (`balance - amount`) to the MPC cluster.

instructions.name: debit_from_account
docs: Spend `amount` from the stored confidential balance with no-overdraft.
The balance is read from the on-chain ciphertext (offset 9), not from the
client — so the client can't claim a balance they don't have. The MPC
enforces `balance >= amount`; the callback writes the new ciphertext back.

accounts.name: confidential_balance
docs: The balance account the callback writes the new ciphertext into.

instructions.name: deposit
docs: Queue a confidential deposit (`balance + amount`) to the MPC cluster.

instructions.name: deposit_to_account
docs: Deposit real tokens into the vault and credit the confidential balance.
`amount` is PUBLIC (the tokens visibly move into the vault), so it is
passed to the circuit as plaintext and the credited amount is bound to
the on-chain transfer — no balance can be minted from thin air. The MPC
adds it to the stored ciphertext; the callback writes the new balance.

accounts.name: owner_token_account
docs: The depositor's token account — tokens move from here into the vault.

accounts.name: vault
docs: The protocol-owned vault holding the real tokens for this mint.

accounts.name: confidential_balance
docs: The balance account the callback writes the new ciphertext into.

instructions.name: init_balance
docs: Create a confidential balance account for (owner, mint) and queue the
MPC computation that fills it with an encrypted zero.

accounts.name: mint
docs: The token mint this confidential balance is for.

accounts.name: confidential_balance
docs: The balance account the callback writes the encrypted zero into.

instructions.name: init_debit_comp_def
docs: One-time: register the `debit` circuit definition with the MXE.

instructions.name: init_deposit_comp_def
docs: One-time: register the `deposit` circuit definition with the MXE.

instructions.name: init_transfer_comp_def
docs: One-time: register the `transfer` circuit definition with the MXE.

instructions.name: init_withdraw_comp_def
docs: One-time: register the `withdraw` circuit definition with the MXE.

instructions.name: initialize_mint
docs: Configure a new SPL mint for confidential custody (creates MintConfig + mint).

instructions.name: reveal_account_balance
docs: Reveal the stored confidential balance (passes the on-chain ciphertext
to the MPC cluster, which decrypts and reveals it).

instructions.name: transfer
docs: Queue a confidential transfer (sender -> receiver) to the MPC cluster.

instructions.name: transfer_between_accounts
docs: Confidential transfer from the signer's balance to the receiver's balance.
Both balances are read from on-chain ciphertext (offset 9) so neither can
be faked; the MPC enforces no-overdraft. The callback writes both new
ciphertexts back.

accounts.name: sender_balance
docs: Sender balance — callback writes the new sender ciphertext here.

accounts.name: receiver_balance
docs: Receiver balance — callback writes the new receiver ciphertext here.

instructions.name: withdraw
docs: Queue a confidential withdraw (`balance - amount`, amount revealed) to
the MPC cluster.

instructions.name: withdraw_from_account
docs: Withdraw a PUBLIC `amount` of real tokens from the vault, debiting the
confidential balance. Two-phase: the MPC checks `balance >= amount` over
the hidden balance and reveals only the released amount; the callback
releases exactly that many tokens (0 if short). The balance is read from
the on-chain ciphertext (offset 9), so it can't be faked.

accounts.name: owner_token_account
docs: Destination for released tokens — must belong to the withdrawer.

accounts.name: confidential_balance
docs: Balance account — callback writes the new ciphertext here.

accounts.name: mint_config
docs: Vault config — supplies the PDA signer seeds and tracks total withdrawn.

accounts.name: vault
docs: Vault token account — released tokens leave here (authority = mint_config).

accounts.name: owner_token_account
docs: Destination token account for the released tokens.

errors.name: InvalidAmount
errors.msg: Amount must be greater than zero

errors.name: InvalidMint
errors.msg: Token account mint does not match the vault mint

types.name: AccountBalanceRevealedEvent
docs: Emitted by `reveal_account_balance_callback` — the revealed stored balance.

types.name: AuditorRevealEvent
docs: Emitted by `reveal_to_auditor_callback` — balance re-encrypted to the auditor.

types.name: BatchTransferEvent
docs: Emitted by `batch_transfer_callback` — sender + 3 receivers' new balances.

types.name: BatchTransferOutput
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.

types.name: ClockAccount
docs: An account storing the current network epoch

types.name: ComputationDefinitionAccount
docs: An account representing a [ComputationDefinition] in a MXE.

types.name: ComputationDefinitionMeta
docs: A computation definition for execution in a MXE.

types.name: ComputationSignature
docs: The signature of a computation defined in a [ComputationDefinition].

types.name: ConfidentialBalance
docs: A confidential, MXE-owned token balance stored on-chain.
`encrypted_balance` is an `Enc<Mxe, u64>` ciphertext — only the MPC cluster
can decrypt it. Deposits/transfers/withdrawals read it into a circuit,
compute on it, and the callback writes the new ciphertext back here.
Keyed by `(owner, mint)` so each token has its own confidential balance.
IMPORTANT: `encrypted_balance` sits immediately after the 8-byte
discriminator + 1-byte bump (offset 9), so the ArgBuilder reads it with
`.account(key, 8 + 1, 32)`. Do not reorder these fields.

fields.name: encrypted_balance
docs: Enc<Mxe, u64> ciphertext (one 32-byte field).

fields.name: nonce
docs: Encryption nonce for `encrypted_balance`.

types.name: DebitEvent
docs: Emitted by `debit_callback` with the new encrypted balance.

fields.name: new_balance
docs: New confidential balance (encrypted; only the owner can decrypt).

types.name: DebitFromAccountOutput
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.

types.name: DebitOutput
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.

types.name: DepositEvent
docs: Emitted by `deposit_callback` with the new encrypted balance.

types.name: DepositMade
docs: Emitted when real tokens are moved into the vault (public on-ramp amount).

types.name: DepositOutput
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.

types.name: DepositToAccountOutput
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.

types.name: Epoch
docs: The network epoch

types.name: InitBalanceOutput
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.

types.name: MXEAccount
docs: A MPC Execution Environment.

types.name: MintConfig
docs: Vault configuration for one SPL mint custodied by the protocol.
The real tokens live in a `vault` token account whose authority is this
PDA. Deposits move tokens in (public amount), withdrawals move them out
(public amount) — only the confidential balances computed in MPC are
private. `total_deposited`/`total_withdrawn` track the public on/off-ramp.

types.name: MintInitialized
docs: Emitted by `initialize_mint` when a new custodied mint is configured.

types.name: MxeStatus
docs: The status of an MXE.

types.name: NodeMetadata
docs: location as [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](https://www.iso.org/iso-3166-country-codes.html) country code

types.name: NodeRef
docs: A reference to a node in the cluster.
The offset is to derive the Node Account.
The current_total_rewards is the total rewards the node has received so far in the current
epoch.

types.name: Output
docs: An output of a computation.
We currently don't support encrypted outputs yet since encrypted values are passed via
data objects.

types.name: Parameter
docs: A parameter of a computation.
We differentiate between plaintext and encrypted parameters and data objects.
Plaintext parameters are directly provided as their value.
Encrypted parameters are provided as an offchain reference to the data.
Data objects are provided as a reference to the data object account.

types.name: ProveThresholdEvent
docs: Emitted by `prove_threshold_callback` — only the boolean solvency result.

types.name: ProveThresholdOutput
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.

types.name: RevealAccountBalanceOutput
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.

types.name: RevealToAuditorOutput
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.

types.name: SetUnset
docs: Utility struct to store a value that needs to be set by a certain number of participants (keys
in our case). Once all participants have set the value, the value is considered set and we only
store it once.

types.name: TransferBetweenAccountsOutput
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.

types.name: TransferEvent
docs: Emitted by `transfer_callback` with both parties' new encrypted balances.

fields.name: new_sender_balance
docs: Sender's new confidential balance (encrypted).

fields.name: new_receiver_balance
docs: Receiver's new confidential balance (encrypted).

types.name: TransferOutput
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.

types.name: ViewBalanceEvent
docs: Emitted by `view_balance_callback` — balance re-encrypted to the owner.

types.name: ViewBalanceOutput
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.

types.name: WithdrawEvent
docs: Emitted by `withdraw_callback` with the new encrypted balance.

types.name: WithdrawFromAccountOutput
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.

types.name: WithdrawOutput
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.

types.name: WithdrawalMade
docs: Emitted when real tokens are released from the vault (public off-ramp amount).
